description: Two hooks. (1) PreToolUse on ExitPlanMode — Plan Briefing checkpoint: a deterministic, stateless Node command hook on EVERY ExitPlanMode (Quick Fix Mode, /create-spec & /update-spec plan phases, and plain plan mode). It allows when a real user reply precedes the call — skipping the agent's mechanical tool turns (e.g. the ToolSearch that loads a deferred ExitPlanMode, a post-approval Edit) so they do not break the check — and blocks a straight planning -> ExitPlanMode skip with a short reminder. The check is anchored to the current plan session: a first pass finds where plan mode was (re-)entered — an EnterPlanMode tool call, or a genuine transition into plan read from a real user message's own permissionMode field (the reliable signal for a manual shift+tab re-entry after a restart drops plan-mode state) — then a second pass walks back only to that boundary, so a deny never points at stale text from a previous plan session or from before a restart. Crucially, a bare permission-mode:plan marker seen WHILE ALREADY in plan is ignored, not treated as a boundary: Claude Code writes one at essentially every plan-mode turn boundary, and the old logic mistook those for a fresh re-entry and false-blocked the compliant briefed-then-replied flow whenever a routine marker sat between the reply and the exit. Fail-open on any uncertainty, so it never deadlocks. Filters subagent (isSidechain) and injected (isMeta) entries. Reads the transcript only; writes nothing anywhere. (2) SessionStart on startup/resume — injects a short reminder (session-start-skill-reminder.js) to load and use the spec-driven-development skill for any code work. Static context injection, no project detection: the reminder itself states the 'if this is a code project' condition and lets the agent judge. Writes nothing.
